briefdescription.para: Pointer to lesser component at a given time, representing G^<(t_lead,t_lead-deltj).
Pointer to retarded component at a given time, representing G^R(t_lead,t_lead-deltj).

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, herm_matrix_moving< T > &G, int delt_g, int j1, int j2)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, herm_matrix_timestep_moving< T > &G, int j1, int j2)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, herm_matrix_timestep_moving_view< T > &G, int j1, int j2)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

briefdescription.para: Returns the number of rows (CurrentlyL always the same as number of columns!).
Writes data to an HDF5 file with a specified filename and group name.
Writes data to an HDF5 file for the specified group ID.
Writes data to an HDF5 file for the specified group ID into a subgroup with specified name.
Class for interfacing with herm_matrix_timestep_moving without copying data.

detaileddescription.para: This class provides an interface to herm_matrix_timestep_moving or herm_matrix_moving storing only pointers to the data instead of owning it. This avoids unnecessary memory duplication but requires careful handling to prevent memory errors due to raw pointer usage.

The herm_matrix_timestep_moving represent a Greens function at physical timestep t_lead. Arguments of the moving timestep are understood relative to t_lead: this->retptr(delt) points to G^ret(t_lead,t_lead-delt), this->lesptr(delt) points to G^les(t_lead,t_lead-delt) for delt=0,...,tc_. The pysical timestep t_lead is not stored

Each element of the Greens function is a size1 x size2 matrix. Currently only size1 = size2 is supported
T
Data type (typically a numerical type like double or complex<double>).
